data.title: Quant check on top retail REIT picks as WSR goes private in $1.7B Ares deal
data.description:
The retail real estate market is shifting following Whitestone REIT’s (WSR) acquisition by Ares Management (ARES), a move that signals the sector remains undervalued. As WSR goes private, investors are now looking to invest in high-performing alternatives within the sector that show better growth, profitability, and valuation metrics.

With WSR exiting the public market, it’s a good time to identify which retail REITs hold the highest "Strong Buy" ratings according to the Seeking Alpha Quant system.

Here is the list: 

Getty Realty (GTY) - Strong Buy (Quant Score: 4.55/5)

Regency Centers Corporation (REG) - Strong Buy (Quant Score: 4.50/5)

NNN REIT (NNN) - Buy (Quant Score: 4.38/5)

Realty Income Corporation (O) - Buy (Quant Score: 4.26/5)

NETSTREIT (NTST) - Buy (Quant Score: 4.20/5)

Kimco Realty (KIM) - Buy (Quant Score: 4.14/5)

Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) - Buy (Quant Score: 3.91/5)

Urban Edge Properties (UE) - Buy (Quant Score: 3.79/5)

FrontView REIT (FVR) - Buy (Quant Score: 3.79/5)

Agree Realty Corporation (ADC) - Buy (Quant Score: 3.73/5)

data.title: New Era Energy & Digital slips 12%, prices $100M stock offering at $3.35 per share
data.description:
• New Era Energy & Digital (NUAI) priced its underwritten public offering of ~29.85M shares of common stock at $3.35 per share, with gross proceeds expected to be $100M
  • The offering is expected to close on April 10.
  • The net proceeds will primarily be used to repay outstanding borrowings under a senior secured convertible promissory note owed to SharonAI, with any remaining funds allocated to general corporate purposes.
  • The underwriters have been granted a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 4.48M shares.
  • The stock price slumped about 12% on Thursday during pre-market trade.

data.title: ImmunityBio reports Q1 results
data.description:
• ImmunityBio press release (IBRX): Q1revenue of $44.2M beats by $1.03M.
  • Preliminary net product revenue of approximately $44.2 million, representing an ~168% year-over-year increase compared with Q1 2025
  • ANKTIVA Unit Growth: 168% increase in unit sales volume in Q1 2026 compared to Q1 2025
  • The Company ended the quarter with an estimated $380.9 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities as of March 31, 2026.

data.title: NioCorp nears deal to sell critical minerals output to Traxys
data.description:
NioCorp Developments (NB) has  entered into a non-binding agreement with Traxys North America LLC outlining a long-term marketing and offtake arrangement for NioCorp's remaining planned critical minerals products from its Elk Creek Critical Minerals Project.

Subject to a definitive contract being executed between NioCorp and Traxys, NioCorp would be positioned to sell out of its planned critical minerals products for the first 10 years of operation, following the successful completion of project financing and commencement of commercial production.

NioCorp has already placed under contract 75% of its planned ferroniobium production and 12% of its planned scandium oxide production.

Traxys could also become a shareholder of NioCorp (NB), while also playing a key role in President Trump's recently announced Project Vault, which aims to facilitate sales of critical minerals between large manufacturers and critical mineral producers.

"Upon execution of definitive agreements with Traxys, we believe that this initiative moves NioCorp closer to securing full project financing for the Elk Creek Project," said Mark A. Smith, CEO, and Executive Chairman of NioCorp.

"Together with our existing offtake agreement with ThyssenKrupp (TYEKF) for 50% of our planned ferroniobium production during the first 10 years of operations, a definitive deal with Traxys would lead to the sale of all our remaining planned products for the first 10 years of operations."

Traxys currently has commercial offtake agreements covering 25% of the company's planned ferroniobium production and up to 12% of its planned scandium production, both over the first 10 years of NioCorp's operations.

Under the proposed offtake agreement, this relationship would be expanded to include an additional 25% of the planned ferroniobium production on a take-or-pay basis and the remaining scandium production, as well as 100% of planned production of all other products, on a best-efforts basis for the first ten years of operations, subject to specified obligations and other terms.

The term sheet also contemplates a potential strategic equity investment by Traxys of up to $30 million in NioCorp, subject to due diligence, negotiation, and execution of definitive agreements, regulatory approvals, and other customary closing conditions.

Traxys is a key participant in Project Vault, an initiative aimed at strengthening U.S. critical minerals supply chains backed by EXIM. NioCorp is also working with EXIM on a potential debt financing package for its Elk Creek Project.

data.title: Iron ore falls after incoming BHP CEO met with Chinalco boss in China
data.description:
Iron ore futures fell to the lowest in a month after Aluminum Corp. of China said its chairman and other executives met with incoming BHP (BHP) CEO Brandon Craig in Beijing this ‌week and discussed further cooperation.

Chinalco Chairman Duan Xiangdong met with Craig on ​Wednesday and ​told ⁠him that he hoped both companies could cooperate further, ​according to a company WeChat ​post.

BHP (BHP) has been embroiled ​in a pricing dispute with its ​biggest customer, China's Mineral Resources Group, which has banned its steel mills from buying several ​products from the Australian company as the two ​work to reach annual supply terms.

A resolution could see restrictions on BHP (BHP) cargoes lifted, expanding supply of the key steelmaking material including ore currently tied up at ports.

According to Bloomberg, iron ore futures (SCO:COM) in Singapore fell as much as 3.5% to $102.10/ton on Thursday, the lowest since March 10, before trimming losses to trade at $102.55/ton, while the Dalian contract dropped as much as 2.4%.

data.title: Blackstone and Dubai Aerospace team up for $1.6B aircraft leasing program
data.description:
Blackstone's (BX) credit and insurance division and Dubai Aerospace Enterprise ((DAE)) agreed to invest in aircraft on lease to commercial airlines, with a target deployment of ~$1.6B annually, the companies said on Thursday.

The global investment program, named Equator, will build a diversified portfolio of commercial aircraft on lease to major airlines around the world. DAE will source the assets from third parties, and DAE's Aircraft Investor Services group will manage the assets owned by Equator.

"This program underscores BXCI’s focus on deploying flexible capital into high-quality investments backed by hard assets," said Aneek Mamik, senior managing director and the head of financial services for asset-based finance at Blackstone Credit & Insurance ((BXCI)).

BXCI expects to offer a spectrum of capital to support the program, enabling flexible and dependable financing solutions across market cycles and investment opportunities, it said. The BXCI investor group will include capital from funds managed by ITE Management LP, a strategic partner of BXCI.

DAE is one of the largest aircraft lessors globally, with ~700 aircraft, including more than 100 aircraft valued at over $4B under management as of Dec. 31, 2025.

BXCI's infrastructure and asset-based credit group manages more than $100B. The platform focuses on providing investment-grade credit, non-investment-grade credit, and structured investments across the real economy, including infrastructure, commercial finance, fund finance, consumer finance, and real estate lending.

Blackstone (BX) stock slipped 0.4% in premarket trading.

data.title: CoreWeave plans $1.25B senior notes offering; shares up ~7%
data.description:
• CoreWeave (CRWV) intends to offer $1,250M aggregate principal amount of senior notes due 2031 in a private offering, it said Thursay. The notes will be guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by certain wholly-owned subsidiaries of CoreWeave.
  • CoreWeave intends to use the proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including, without limitation, repayment of outstanding indebtedness, and to pay fees, costs and expenses in connection with the offering of the notes. The company's shares rose about 7% in early trading. 
  • Separately, CoreWeave (CRWV) inked an expanded, long-term agreement with Meta Platforms (META) to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for approximately $21 billion. With this deal, the two companies are continuing their existing relationship, increasing support for Meta’s development and deployment of AI.

data.title: CoreWeave, Meta enter into a $21B expanded AI infrastructure pact
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) shares jumped 7% premarket on Thursday after it announced an expanded, long-term pact with Meta Platforms (META) to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for ~$21B.

With this deal, the two companies are continuing their existing relationship, increasing support for Meta’s development and deployment of AI. Meta (META) will leverage CoreWeave’s (CRWV) AI cloud platform to scale inference workloads.

“This is another example that leading companies are choosing CoreWeave’s AI cloud to run their most demanding workloads,” said Michael Intrator, co-founder, CEO, chairman of CoreWeave.

CRWV +7.5% premarket to $95.61

Source: Press Release

data.title: Blackberry Non-GAAP EPS of $0.06 beats by $0.02, revenue of $156M beats by $11.45M
data.description:

data.title: Amazon's chip unit's annual revenue run rate over $20B, discusses capex returns
data.description:
Amazon's (AMZN) CEO Andy Jassy discussed the company's chip business and returns on the company's massive $200B capital expenditure plans for 2026, and other things in his 2025 letter to shareholders.

"We’re not investing approximately $200 billion in capex in 2026 on a hunch. The recent OpenAI (OPENAI) commitment (over $100 billion) is an example of this, but there are several other customer agreements completed (and unannounced), or deep in process. Of the AWS capex we expect to spend in 2026, much of which will be monetized in 2027-2028, we already have customer commitments for a substantial portion of it," said Jassy in the letter, which was out on Thursday.

During the company's fourth-quarter earnings call in February, Jassy said that the company expects to invest about $200B in capital expenditures, or capex, across Amazon, but predominantly in Amazon Web Services, or AWS. He added that the company has very high demand and customers want AWS for core and AI workloads, and "we're monetizing capacity as fast as we can install it."

Jassy said in the letter that the annual revenue run rate for the company's chips business (inclusive of Graviton, Trainium, and Nitro) is now over $20B and growing triple-digit percentages year-over-year.

"To dimensionalize this versus other chips companies, that run rate is somewhat understated by our currently only monetizing our chips through EC2. If our chips business was a stand-alone business, and sold chips produced this year to AWS and other third parties (as other leading chips companies do), our annual run rate would be ~$50 billion. There’s so much demand for our chips that it’s quite possible we’ll sell racks of them to third parties in the future," Jassy noted.

Jassy added that at scale, Amazon expects the Trainium AI chips will save it tens of billions of capex dollars per year and provide several hundred basis points of operating margin advantage versus relying on others' chips for inference.

AI inference is the process of running a trained AI model to produce predictions or conclusions on new, unseen data.

Jassy noted that Trainium3, which started shipping at the start of 2026 and is 30% to 40% more price-performant than Trainium2, is nearly fully subscribed.

"A significant chunk of Trainium4, which is still about 18 months from broad availability, has already been reserved. And, Amazon Bedrock, AWS’s primary (and very fast-growing) inference service, runs most of its inference on Trainium. Demand for Trainium is booming," Jassy noted.

Jassy spoke about the company's partnership with Nvidia (NVDA) but also about competition.

"We have a strong partnership with NVIDIA, will always have customers who choose to run NVIDIA, and we will continue to make AWS the best place to run NVIDIA. However, customers want better price-performance," Jassy noted.

Jassy said that the second version of its custom AI silicon, Trainium2, had about 30% better price-performance than comparable graphics processing units, or GPUs, and has largely sold out.

In addition, Jassy said that AWS added 3.9 gigawatts, or GW, of new power capacity in 2025, expects to double total power capacity by the end of 2027, and is monetizing that capacity as fast as it is installed. He added that in the fourth quarter of 2025, AWS reported 24% year-over-year growth with a $142B revenue run rate.

"That’s a lot of absolute growth. And yet, we still have capacity constraints that yield unserved demand. [As an aside, two large AWS customers have already asked if they could buy *all* of our Graviton instance capacity in 2026 (Graviton is our widely-adopted custom CPU chip)—we can’t agree to these requests given other customers’ needs, but it gives you an idea of the demand.]," said Jassy.

Shares of Amazon were largely flat but in the green premarket on Thursday.
